text: Could I get a medium latte {{c1::to go}}, please?
explanation: to go is used when you want to take your food or drink out of the shop instead of consuming it there.
usage:
Pattern: Could I get a [size] [drink] to go, please?
  • Could I get a large Americano to go, please?
  • Could I get a small espresso to go, please?
spanish: ¿Me da un latte mediano para llevar, por favor?

text: I'd like to {{c1::substitute}} whole milk {{c1::with}} oat milk, please.
explanation: substitute A with B means to use B instead of A. This is the standard way to customize food or drink orders.
usage:
Pattern: substitute [original] with [alternative]
  • Can I substitute white bread with wheat bread?
  • I'd like to substitute fries with a side salad, please.
spanish: Me gustaría cambiar la leche entera por leche de avena, por favor.

text: Do you have any {{c1::sugar-free}} syrup options?
explanation: sugar-free means containing no sugar. Syrups are sweet liquid flavorings added to coffee (like vanilla or caramel).
usage:
Pattern: [adjective]-free (e.g., gluten-free, caffeine-free, tax-free)
  • Is this cookie gluten-free?
  • I'd prefer decaf, do you have caffeine-free coffee?
spanish: ¿Tienen opciones de jarabe sin azúcar?

text: Can I {{c1::pay by card}}?
explanation: pay by card means to pay using a credit or debit card rather than cash.
usage:
Pattern: pay by [method] (e.g., pay by card, pay by phone, pay by check) but pay in cash
  • You can pay by phone using Apple Pay.
  • Is it cheaper if I pay in cash?
spanish: ¿Puedo pagar con tarjeta?

text: I would like to order a large cold brew with a {{c1::splash of}} oat milk.
explanation: A 'splash of' is an informal measurement meaning a very small amount of liquid (typically milk, cream, or water) added to a drink.
usage:
Pattern: splash of [liquid]
  • Just a splash of milk in my tea, please.
  • He likes his whiskey with a splash of soda.
spanish: Me gustaría pedir un café extraído en frío grande con un chorrito de leche de avena.

text: Do you have any {{c1::dairy-free}} options for the latte?
explanation: 'Dairy-free' refers to foods or beverages that contain no milk or milk derivatives, suitable for lactose-intolerant or vegan customers.
usage:
Pattern: dairy-free milk / plant-based milk
  • Soy and almond milk are common dairy-free alternatives.
  • Is this caramel syrup dairy-free?
spanish: ¿Tienen alguna opción sin lácteos para el café con leche?

text: I'll have a double espresso, and can I get that {{c1::sweetened with}} honey?
explanation: To get something 'sweetened with' means to add a specific sweetener (like honey, sugar, syrup, or stevia) to a drink or food.
usage:
Pattern: sweetened with [sweetener]
  • I prefer my tea sweetened with raw sugar.
  • This lemonade is sweetened with agave nectar.
spanish: Tomaré un espresso doble, ¿y me lo pueden dar endulzado con miel?

text: Is there a password for the {{c1::guest Wi-Fi}} here?
explanation: 'Guest Wi-Fi' is a secondary wireless network provided by businesses for clients and visitors, keeping the internal network secure.
usage:
Pattern: guest Wi-Fi / public network
  • The guest Wi-Fi is free for customers who buy a drink.
  • Ask the barista for the guest Wi-Fi password.
spanish: ¿Hay contraseña para el Wi-Fi para invitados aquí?

text: I'd like to buy a bag of whole bean coffee, could you {{c1::grind it for}} drip?
explanation: To 'grind it for [method]' means to crush whole coffee beans into the specific particle size required for a brewing method (e.g. drip, espresso, french press).
usage:
Pattern: grind [coffee] for [brewing method]
  • Could you grind these beans for a French press?
  • I grind my own coffee beans for espresso every morning.
spanish: Me gustaría comprar una bolsa de café en grano entero, ¿podrían molerlo para cafetera de filtro?

text: Can I get a pastry to go, and please {{c1::warm it up}} for me?
explanation: To 'warm up' something means to make it warm or hot, usually by putting it in a microwave, oven, or toaster.
usage:
Pattern: warm [something] up / heat up
  • Would you like me to warm up your croissant?
  • I need to warm up my lunch in the microwave.
spanish: ¿Me da una factura/pan dulce para llevar, y por favor me la calienta?

text: I'll have a decaf latte, and please make sure it is {{c1::extra hot}}.
explanation: 'Extra hot' is a specific order request asking the barista to steam the milk to a higher temperature (typically around 160-170°F).
usage:
Pattern: extra hot / steam the milk extra hot
  • Could you make my mocha extra hot?
  • I like my cappuccino extra hot when it is freezing outside.
spanish: Tomaré un café con leche descafeinado, y por favor asegúrese de que esté extra caliente.

text: Do you have any sugar substitutes like stevia or {{c1::artificial sweeteners}}?
explanation: An 'artificial sweetener' is a food additive that provides a sweet taste like that of sugar while containing significantly less food energy.
usage:
Pattern: artificial sweeteners / sugar substitutes
  • I don't use sugar; I prefer artificial sweeteners.
  • Does this coffee shop have any artificial sweeteners like Splenda?
spanish: ¿Tienen algún sustituto de azúcar como stevia o edulcorantes artificiales?

text: I'd like to sit outside; is there any table available on the {{c1::patio}}?
explanation: A 'patio' (or terrace) is an outdoor space adjacent to a cafe or house, typically paved and used for dining or recreation.
usage:
Pattern: patio / outdoor seating / terrace
  • Let's sit on the patio to enjoy the sunny weather.
  • The patio was packed with customers.
spanish: Me gustaría sentarme afuera; ¿hay alguna mesa disponible en la terraza/patio?

text: Can I get a slice of banana bread and please {{c1::toast it}} for me?
explanation: To 'toast' a pastry means to heat it using a toaster or grill until it is warm and slightly crispy on the outside.
usage:
Pattern: toast it / toasted bread
  • Would you like me to toast the bagel for you?
  • I prefer my bread lightly toasted.
spanish: ¿Me da una rebanada de pan de plátano y por favor me la tuesta?

text: I'll buy this mug; does it come with a lid or is it sold {{c1::as is}}?
explanation: 'As is' is a legal and commercial phrase meaning that the product is sold in its current condition, with all faults, without any warranty.
usage:
Pattern: sold as is / buy as is
  • The defective device was sold as is with no returns.
  • We bought the display model as is for a discount.
spanish: Compraré esta taza; ¿viene con tapa o se vende tal como está?

text: Excuse me, where can I find some napkins and {{c1::stirrers}}?
explanation: A 'stirrer' is a small wooden or plastic stick used to mix sugar or milk into a hot beverage like coffee or tea.
usage:
Pattern: wooden stirrers / plastic stirrers
  • Wooden stirrers are more eco-friendly than plastic ones.
  • Please grab a stirrer from the condiment counter.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿dónde puedo encontrar algunas servilletas y agitadores?

text: I'd like to purchase some coffee beans; which blend do you {{c1::recommend for}} espresso?
explanation: To 'recommend for [method]' is the standard collocation used when asking a barista or merchant which product matches a specific preparation method.
usage:
Pattern: recommend for [brewing method / use case]
  • Which coffee beans do you recommend for cold brew?
  • The barista recommended this dark roast for espresso.
spanish: Me gustaría comprar algunos granos de café; ¿qué mezcla me recomienda para espresso?

text: Could you add some cocoa powder or cinnamon on {{c1::top of}} the foam?
explanation: 'On top of' is a prepositional phrase meaning directly on the upper surface of something.
usage:
Pattern: on top of [surface / foam]
  • Sprinkle some chocolate flakes on top of the cream.
  • He put his keys on top of the table.
spanish: ¿Podría agregar un poco de cacao en polvo o canela encima de la espuma?

text: Do you offer free refills on drip coffee if I {{c1::dine in}}?
explanation: To 'dine in' (or eat in) means to consume your food or drink inside the restaurant or cafe premises, as opposed to taking it 'to go'.
usage:
Pattern: dine in / eat in / to go
  • Would you like to dine in or is this to go?
  • Dine-in customers get free water refills.
spanish: ¿Ofrecen recargas gratuitas de café de filtro si consumo aquí?

text: I'll pay with my phone using the contactless {{c1::payment reader}}.
explanation: A 'payment reader' (or card terminal) is the electronic device that scans credit cards or mobile wallets using NFC for transactions.
usage:
Pattern: payment reader / card reader / card terminal
  • Hold your phone near the payment reader to complete the transaction.
  • The payment reader is not responding.
spanish: Pagaré con mi teléfono usando el lector de pagos sin contacto.
